title: Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions
description: Tag questions: the little checkers at the end of a sentence. 'It's hot, isn't it?' 'You like sushi, don't you?' Rules and rhythm.
summary_pt_br: Question tags em inglês: a perguntinha no fim da frase ('isn't it?', 'don't you?', 'aren't they?'). Regra: afirmação pede tag NEGATIVA, negação pede tag POSITIVA. Auxiliar espelha o verbo principal.

scenes.text_en:
Zara tilted her head. 'The coffee is cold, ISN'T IT?'
'You enjoy surfing, DON'T YOU?'
'They haven't arrived, HAVE THEY?'
'You aren't tired, ARE YOU?'
Four sentences, four tags. Flip the polarity, match the auxiliary.
scenes.text_pt:
Zara inclinou a cabeça. 'O café está frio, NÃO ESTÁ?'
'Você gosta de surf, NÃO GOSTA?'
'Eles não chegaram, CHEGARAM?'
'Você não está cansado, ESTÁ?'
Quatro frases, quatro tags. Inverte a polaridade, espelha o auxiliar.

scenes.title: Two rules for tag questions
scenes.body_en: RULE 1 — Flip the polarity: positive statement → NEGATIVE tag. Negative statement → POSITIVE tag. 'She's happy, ISN'T she?' / 'She isn't happy, IS she?' RULE 2 — Match the auxiliary. 'She IS' → 'isn't she?'. 'They HAVE gone' → 'haven't they?'. 'You LIKE it' (no auxiliary) → use DO: 'don't you?'.
scenes.body_pt: Duas regras: (1) Inverter polaridade — afirmativa pede tag negativa, e vice-versa. (2) Espelhar o auxiliar do verbo principal. Se não tem auxiliar, use do/does/did.

examples.pt: A gente deveria ir, não deveria?

exercise.question: Complete: 'Maya is smart, ___ she?'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Afirmativa pede tag negativa. is → isn't she.

exercise.question: 'They don't eat meat, ___?'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Negativa pede tag positiva. don't → do they.

exercise.question: Complete: 'You've been to Rio, ___ you?' (present perfect)
exercise.explanation: 've been = have been → tag uses HAVE. Positive → negative = haven't you.

turns.pt: Você gosta de cappuccino, não gosta?

turns.en: I do. You don't drink coffee, do you?
turns.pt: Gosto. Você não toma café, toma?

exercise.explanation: Five tag patterns. Notice the polarity flip every time.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco tags. Sempre inverte a polaridade.
